keep.reason: More comprehensive - checks all fields that log_ids_ips_enabled_if_supported checks (LogDestinationType=cloud-watch-logs, LogFormat exists) plus DeliverLogsStatus=SUCCESS

remove.reason: Subset of alerts_for_anomalies_configured - checks only LogDestinationType=cloud-watch-logs and LogFormat exists, which are already covered by alerts_for_anomalies_configured

recommendations.description: Merge log_ids_ips_enabled_if_supported into alerts_for_anomalies_configured - log_ids_ips_enabled_if_supported checks LogDestinationType=cloud-watch-logs and LogFormat exists, which are already covered by alerts_for_anomalies_configured (which also checks DeliverLogsStatus=SUCCESS).
recommendations.impact: Reduces duplicate checks, improves maintainability. All compliance coverage preserved.

yaml_fixes.issue: Client was set to 'vpcflowlogs' but should be 'ec2' since VPC Flow Logs use EC2 client in boto3
yaml_fixes.note: Discovery correctly uses aws.ec2.describe_flow_logs, so client should match

validation_summary.notes: All discoveries correctly use items_for for arrays. All field paths match emit structure. Test executed successfully with no errors. All 4 rules are properly configured. Fixed client setting from 'vpcflowlogs' to 'ec2' to match the discovery method and boto3 service mapping. Note: 0 checks executed because no VPC Flow Logs exist in the accounts.
